frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis / classification
frameworks.one_line: Decode stimulus color from spatially distributed fMRI voxel activity.

frameworks.name: forward encoding model of color tuning
frameworks.one_line: Idealized color-tuning channels reconstruct novel untrained colors.

frameworks.name: principal component analysis
frameworks.one_line: Derive perceptual color space from voxel response covariation.

core_question: How is color represented by spatially distributed patterns of activity in human visual cortex?
key_assumptions: Color information is carried by distributed voxel patterns, not mean area response
Idealized color-tuning channels can model voxel responses linearly
manipulations: Stimulus color across multiple chromatic values
Cortical area (V1, V2, V3, V4, VO1, LO1, LO2, V3A/B, MT+)
Analysis method (classifier vs forward model vs PCA)
key_findings: Stimulus color was accurately decoded from V1, V2, V3, V4, VO1 but not LO1/LO2/V3A/B/MT+
Forward model matched classifier accuracy and additionally reconstructed novel untrained colors
Mean voxel-averaged responses were not reliably distinguishable across colors
In V4 and VO1, first two PC scores traced a progression through perceptual color space
interpretations: Each color is associated with a unique distributed activity pattern across early/ventral visual cortex
V4 and VO1 represent color in a perceptually organized space, unlike V1 despite V1's high decoding accuracy
